spec.reasoning: This task references many specific cell addresses (E19, E5, E7, E9, F10, F7), so any row/column shifting (blank_row_insert, title_row_insert, column_reorder) would break the cell references and change the task logic. The header is already not in row 1 (it's in row 2), further confirming we must avoid layout shifts. Instead, I use two safe perturbations: (1) Rename 'Open AEW' to 'Active AEW' — a semantically equivalent abbreviation-level rename for a column not referenced in the instruction, which tests whether the model hardcodes column names. (2) Add a distractor column 'MI Period' after the last column — this is plausibly related to the task (middle initial period) but is clearly a different column from any existing one, testing whether the model gets confused and writes to the wrong location. No data_value_renames are needed since 'Open AEW' does not appear as data values elsewhere. The answer position F7 remains unchanged.
